decks.notes: The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly and efficiently control the battlefield by playing aggressive creatures and spells that can deal damage to the opponent while also disrupting their game plan. The deck aims to apply constant pressure through a combination of cheap spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, alongside efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler. By utilizing cards like Boomerang Basics and Expressive Iteration, the deck can maintain card advantage and tempo, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive game plan.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive creature plays with the ability to repeatedly blink or flicker them for added value. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck can protect its creatures, disrupt opponents, and generate card advantage. The deck also includes powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk to close out games quickly. Additionally, cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a mix of aggression and disruptive plays.

The main idea behind this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with quick and aggressive creature plays, backed up by burn spells and disruptive enchantments. The deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to deal damage quickly, while cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge provide additional reach and removal. The inclusion of cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison aims to disrupt the opponent's mana base and game plan, allowing the deck to maintain control of the board and finish the game swiftly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with artifacts like Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal, while utilizing cards like Urza's Saga and Engineered Explosives to create a threatening board presence. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Metallic Rebuke and Tormod's Crypt to control the opponent's game plan. Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides card advantage and recursion, while Pinnacle Emissary and Salvage Titan offer powerful finishers. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggro creatures and disruptive artifacts.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly using Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga to ramp into powerful threats like Emrakul, the Promised End. It also utilizes combo pieces like Glaring Fleshraker and Kozilek's Command to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The key strategy is to assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with big threats while maintaining board control through removal spells like Dismember and Malevolent Rumble. By utilizing a mix of ramp, control, and combo elements, the deck aims to create a fast and powerful game plan to secure victory.

The deck aims to quickly establish a board presence and overwhelm the opponent with efficient and aggressive plays. It utilizes artifacts to generate mana and trigger various synergies, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Repeal and Lightning Bolt. The deck is focused on generating value and card advantage through Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, while also presenting a threat with creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter and Vivi Ornitier. Urza's Saga is a key card that helps to tutor for important artifacts and provide additional resources.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal, and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition. By efficiently managing resources and manipulating the board state, the goal is to outvalue the opponent and eventually close out the game with powerful threats like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Orcish Bowmasters. With a mix of disruption, card draw, and card advantage, this Dimir Control deck aims to outlast and outsmart its opponent in order to secure victory.

This Rakdos Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost creatures and pump spells to deal a large amount of damage in a short amount of time. The deck aims to be fast and aggressive, utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Callous Sell-Sword to pressure the opponent early on, while also using pump spells like Mutagenic Growth and Leyline of Resonance to maximize damage output. The deck also includes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Slickshot Show-Off to provide additional value and reach. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to be fast, aggressive, and efficient in dealing damage to the opponent.

The Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and burn spells. Cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls provide early pressure, while Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge offer reach and removal. The deck also includes support cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer to generate card advantage and maintain pressure. With a mix of aggressive creatures, burn, and resilience, the Boros Aggro deck aims to close out games quickly while maintaining a steady stream of threats.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful combo using cards like Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, and Blade of the Bloodchief to generate massive amounts of value and overwhelm opponents. By utilizing Eldrazi creatures and various support cards, the goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of cards like Emrakul, the Promised End and Malevolent Rumble provide additional win conditions and removal options, making the deck versatile and capable of adapting to different situations. Additionally, the deck utilizes Urza's Saga to search for key pieces and maintain a strong board presence.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with a mix of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge. Utilizing cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos for added utility and disruption, the deck aims to maintain board presence and apply pressure early on. The inclusion of cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison further disrupt opponents' strategies, while cards like Seasoned Pyromancer provide card advantage and token generation. The deck's strategy is to close out games swiftly with efficient threats and disruptive spells.

The deck focuses on using Amulet of Vigor to generate mana quickly and efficiently, allowing for explosive plays with Primeval Titan and Scapeshift. By utilizing lands that produce multiple mana or have additional abilities, the deck can search for key pieces like Urza's Saga and Boseiju, Who Endures. The goal is to ramp up mana quickly, tutor for combo pieces with Tolaria West, and overwhelm opponents with powerful threats like Primeval Titan and Cultivator Colossus. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Zuran Orb and Green Sun's Zenith provide additional utility and flexibility in various situations.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly and efficiently overwhelming the opponent with a combination of low-cost creatures and spells. With cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, the deck aims to put constant pressure on the opponent while also having some control elements like Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison to disrupt their game plan. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker to secure victories in the late game. Overall, the deck is aggressive and versatile, capable of adapting to different matchups.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand, then using cards like Goryo's Vengeance to bring them back onto the battlefield for a powerful instant reanimation combo. It also utilizes control elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The versatility of cards like Ephemerate and Faithful Mending allow for card advantage and protection of key combo pieces. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with unexpected plays and powerful creature reanimation.

The key concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. By using cards like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Spell Snare, the deck aims to control the board and prevent the opponent from resolving key threats. The deck also utilizes card draw spells like Consult the Star Charts and Cling to Dust to maintain a steady stream of resources. Additionally, the deck includes powerful finishers like Sheoldred's Edict and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian to close out the game once the opponent's resources are depleted.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast, aggressive gameplay with a mix of creatures and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain that pressure throughout the game, using efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck also has some utility cards like Mana Tithe and Thraben Charm to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the strategy is to be proactive and close out the game quickly.
